title: Shirk (Polytheism)
consequences: Dying upon Major Shirk guarantees eternal damnation in the Hellfire. All life's good deeds (charity, prayer, fasting) are rendered completely void and useless on the Day of Judgment.
reasonsToAvoid: Allah created humanity solely for His worship. Ascribing His absolute power, sustenance, or right to be worshipped to creation is the ultimate injustice and betrayal of the Creator.
howToFix: To fix Shirk, one must repent sincerely before death by making Tawbah. One must renew their Shahadah (Testimony of Faith), abandon the polytheistic practices immediately, and resolve never to return to them.
evidenceQuran: Indeed, Allah does not forgive association with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Shall I not inform you of the greatest of the major sins?" They said, "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." He said, "Associating partners with Allah..."

title: Zina (Fornication & Adultery)
consequences: Spiritual death of the heart. In the Hereafter, severe punishments await unless repented. In society, it leads to broken homes, illegitimacy, spread of diseases, and the destruction of family lineage.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys the sanctity of marriage and family, which is the foundational block of society. It prioritizes temporary animalistic desires over spiritual purity and loyalty.
howToFix: Immediate and sincere Tawbah (repentance). Breaking off all contact with the illicit partner. Masking the sin (not exposing oneself). Engaging heavily in good deeds to wipe out the bad, and seeking lawful marriage.
evidenceQuran: And do not approach unlawful sexual intercourse. Indeed, it is ever an immorality and is evil as a way.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "When a person commits Zina, faith leaves him and hovers over him like a canopy. When he quits that act, faith returns to him."

title: Riba (Usury/Interest)
description: A Declaration of War Against Allah
consequences: Consuming Riba brings a curse upon one's wealth, destroying its blessing (Barakah). Allah and His Messenger declare war against those who consume Riba. It leads to eternal punishment in the Hereafter.
reasonsToAvoid: Riba exploits the poor by making the rich richer without any productive risk. It creates systemic economic inequality, debt slavery, and removes charity and compassion from society.
howToFix: Cease all interest-bearing transactions immediately. If bound by a contract, pay off the principal as fast as possible to escape it. Any accrued interest earned must be given away to charity without the intention of reward, purely to dispose of it.
evidenceQuran: O you who have believed, fear Allah and give up what remains [due to you] of interest, if you should be believers. And if you do not, then be informed of a war [against you] from Allah and His Messenger.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) cursed the one who consumes Riba, the one who pays it, the one who writes it down, and the two who witness it, and he said: "They are all the same."

title: Sihr (Magic & Sorcery)
consequences: Practicing magic takes a Muslim out of the fold of Islam. In this life, it brings darkness, paranoia, and ruin. In the Hereafter, the punishment is eternal Hellfire, as they have sold their faith for worldly gain.
reasonsToAvoid: It requires bowing down to evil forces (devils) instead of Allah. It causes immense, unjust harm to innocent people, destroys marriages, and shatters faith.
howToFix: A magician must repent sincerely, destroy all magical items, and never return to it. A victim of magic should seek cure through Ruqyah Shariyah (reciting Quran, particularly Al-Baqarah, Al-Falaq, and An-Nas) and never counter magic with magic.
evidenceQuran: But the devils disbelieved, teaching people magic... And they [i.e., people] learn what harms them and does not benefit them. But they certainly knew that whoever purchased the magic would not have in the Hereafter any share.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever goes to a fortune-teller and believes what he says, has disbelieved in what was revealed to Muhammad."

title: Gheebah (Backbiting)
description: Eating the Flesh of Your Brother
consequences: In the grave, severe torment. On the Day of Judgment, the backbiter will be forced to give their hard-earned good deeds away to the victims until they have none left, leading them to Hell.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys communal trust, brotherhood, and love. It breeds hatred, suspicion, and societal fragmentation. It focuses on the flaws of others instead of one's own need for self-improvement.
howToFix: Stop immediately. Seek forgiveness from Allah. If possible without causing greater harm, seek forgiveness from the person you backbit. If telling them would cause hatred, then make abundant Dua for them and praise them in their absence to undo the damage.
evidenceQuran: And do not spy or backbite each other. Would one of you like to eat the flesh of his brother when dead? You would detest it.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) asked: "Do you know what backbiting is?" They said, "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said, "Mentioning your brother with what he dislikes." Someone asked, "What if it is true?" The Prophet said, "If it is true, you have backbitten him. If it is false, you have slandered him."

title: Defining Jihad
description: Striving in the Path of Allah
consequences: Abandoning the spiritual struggle leads to moral decay. Misapplying physical Jihad leads to terrorism, innocent bloodshed, and immense sin. Righteous Jihad brings the highest rewards and elevations in Paradise.
reasonsToAvoid: One must absolutely avoid the "extremist" interpretation of Jihad (vigilantism, targeting non-combatants, rebellion against states). In physical warfare, it is strictly forbidden to kill women, children, the elderly, monks, or destroy trees and infrastructure.
howToFix: Correcting misconceptions requires seeking knowledge from verified, classical scholars. Ensure your daily Jihad is focused on fighting your own sins, establishing prayer, giving charity, and speaking truth with wisdom.
evidenceQuran: And strive for Allah with the striving due to Him. He has chosen you and has not placed upon you in the religion any difficulty.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The greatest Jihad is to speak a word of truth to a tyrant ruler."

title: Aqeedah (Creed)
consequences: Having an incorrect or corrupted Aqeedah can render one's good deeds completely useless, as the foundation of faith is fundamentally flawed.
reasonsToAvoid: Following desires, blindly following forefathers without evidence, or listening to philosophical sects that deviate from the Quran and authentic Sunnah.
howToFix: Study the classical texts of Aqeedah (like Aqeedah al-Tahawiyyah) and adhere closely to the understanding of the Salaf (the early righteous generations).
evidenceQuran: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and [so have] the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers...
evidenceHadith: It is narrated on the authority of Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) that the Prophet (SAW) said: "Islam is built upon five pillars..."

title: Death & The Grave
consequences: Dying upon disbelief or unrepented major sins leads to unimaginable torment in the grave (Adhab al-Qabr), squeezing of the grave, and punishment until the Day of Judgment.
reasonsToAvoid: Forgetting death leads to attachment to the worldly life (Dunya), accumulating sins without repentance, and wasting the limited time given to prepare for eternity.
howToFix: Remember death often as the "destroyer of pleasures". Visit the graves to reflect. Pray correctly and consistently to be able to answer the grave's angels. Recite Surah Al-Mulk every night for protection from the punishment of the grave.
evidenceQuran: Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial; and to Us you will be returned.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Remember often the destroyer of pleasures (death)."

title: Qiyamah (Day of Judgment)
consequences: Those whose bad deeds outweigh their good will fall into Jahannam (Hell). Those whose good deeds are heavier will enter Jannah (Paradise). No injustice will be done to a single soul.
reasonsToAvoid: Ignoring the reality of this Day leads to oppression, arrogance, and a life lived purely for worldly desires without accountability.
howToFix: Ensure you owe no debts to people (whether financial or moral), as rights of creation will be settled on that day before rights of Allah. Ask for forgiveness constantly and perform sincere good deeds.
evidenceQuran: So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it, And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The people will be gathered on the Day of Resurrection, barefoot, naked, and uncircumcised."

title: Bid'ah (Religious Innovation)
consequences: The Prophet (SAW) warned that every Bid'ah leads to the Fire. Those who invent and practice Bid'ah will be turned away from the Prophet's fountain (Al-Hawd) on the Day of Judgment.
reasonsToAvoid: It corrupts the pure religion of Islam, fragments the Ummah into sects, and shifts focus away from the immense, authentic Sunnahs that are already established.
howToFix: Learn the Sunnah correctly. Do not act upon religious rituals that cannot be traced back to authentic Hadith or the Prophet's practice. Abandon cultural practices that masquerade as religion.
evidenceQuran: This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever introduces something into this affair of ours that does not belong to it will have it rejected."

title: Nifaq (Hypocrisy)
consequences: The hypocrites (Major) will be cast into the absolute lowest, most severe level of Hellfire (Jahannam), beneath even the idol worshippers.
reasonsToAvoid: Hypocrisy leads to a life built on lies, paranoia, and cowardice. It ruins communities by creating internal deception and backstabbing.
howToFix: Purify intentions so that all actions are for Allah alone in private and in public. Uphold honesty strictly. The true believers always fear hypocrisy for themselves; only a hypocrite feels entirely safe from it.
evidenceQuran: Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire - and never will you find for them a helper.
evidenceHadith: The signs of a hypocrite are three: Whenever he speaks, he tells a lie; whenever he promises, he breaks it; and whenever he is trusted, he betrays.

title: Kibr (Arrogance & Pride)
consequences: Whoever has an atom's weight of arrogance in their heart will not enter Paradise. On the Day of Judgment, the arrogant will be resurrected the size of ants, stepped on by the masses in humiliation.
reasonsToAvoid: It blinds a person from their own faults. It destroys relationships and makes learning impossible because an arrogant person believes they know it all.
howToFix: Embrace deep humility (Tawadu'). Recognize your origins (from dust and a drop of fluid) and your inevitable end (a corpse returning to dust). Serve the poor and listen to advice gracefully.
evidenceQuran: And do not turn your cheek [in contempt] toward people and do not walk through the earth exultantly. Indeed, Allah does not like everyone self-deluded and boastful.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "He who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of pride shall not enter Paradise."

title: Disobedience to Parents
consequences: It is one of the greatest of the Major Sins. It accelerates punishment in this world before the Hereafter. A person who dies while their parents are angry with them risks being denied entry to Paradise.
reasonsToAvoid: Parents sacrificed their youth, sleep, and wealth to raise you. Disrespecting them is the ultimate form of ingratitude. A society that disrespects its elders inevitably collapses.
howToFix: If they are alive, seek their forgiveness, serve them, lower the "wing of humility" to them, and spend on them. If they have passed away, make constant Dua for them, give charity on their behalf, and maintain ties with their friends and relatives.
evidenceQuran: And your Lord has decreed that you not worship except Him, and to parents, good treatment. Whether one or both of them reach old age [while] with you, say not to them [so much as], "uff," and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) was asked about the major sins. He said: "Associating partners with Allah, and disobedience to parents..."

title: Abandoning Salah (Prayer)
consequences: Severe punishment in the grave. In the Hereafter, they will be resurrected with Pharaoh and Haman. In Jahannam, there is a specific valley called Saqar prepared explicitly for those who did not pray.
reasonsToAvoid: Prayer is the direct lifeline to the Creator. Abandoning it cuts off one's soul from oxygen, leaving the person exposed to Shaytaan, anxiety, depression, and spiritual ruin.
howToFix: Start praying immediately. Acknowledge the immense sin of the missed prayers. Make sincere Tawbah and heavily engage in voluntary (Sunnah and Nafl) prayers to make up for the deficiencies.
evidenceQuran: [They will be in] gardens, questioning each other, About the criminals, [And asking them], "What put you into Saqar (Hell)?" They will say, "We were not of those who prayed..."
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The covenant that distinguishes between us and them is prayer; so whoever leaves it, he has disbelieved."

title: Khamr (Intoxicants & Alcohol)
consequences: Whoever dies addicted to intoxicants without repentance will be made to drink the boiling pus and sweat of the inhabitants of Hellfire (Teenat al-Khabal). In this world, it destroys intellect, families, and wealth.
reasonsToAvoid: The intellect is a sacred gift from Allah separating humans from animals. Intoxicants deliberately destroy this gift, leading to murder, Zina, and the neglect of family.
howToFix: Immediate cessation and repentance. One must endure the withdrawal, seek righteous company, throw away all forbidden substances, and turn to Allah to heal the spiritual void the addiction attempted to fill.
evidenceQuran: O you who have believed, indeed, intoxicants, gambling, [sacrificing on] stone altars [to other than Allah], and divining arrows are but defilement from the work of Satan, so avoid it that you may be successful.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Every intoxicant is Khamr, and every intoxicant is Haram."

title: Maysir (Gambling & Betting)
consequences: Wealth acquired through gambling is filthy. Spending it on food makes the body fueled by Haraam, meaning one's Duas will not be accepted. It ruins households entirely.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys the ethics of honest work and creates an addiction that mimics drug dependency, leading to theft, suicide, and broken families.
howToFix: Stop gambling globally. If one has won money through gambling, they must dispose of it by giving it to the poor without expecting any spiritual reward (merely to clean themselves of it). Seek treatment for the addiction.
evidenceQuran: Satan only wants to cause between you animosity and hatred through intoxicants and gambling and to avert you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. So will you not desist?
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever says to his companion, 'Come, let me gamble with you,' must give charity (as expiation for the mere thought/invite)."

title: Withholding Zakat
consequences: The hoarded wealth will be heated in the fire of Hell and used to brand the foreheads, flanks, and backs of those who withheld it. Or, it will take the shape of a bald venomous snake that will bite their faces continuously.
reasonsToAvoid: Wealth ultimately belongs to Allah. Zakat is the "right of the poor" within the wealth of the rich. Hoarding it creates extreme poverty, societal unrest, and showcases a complete lack of reliance on Allah (Tawakkul).
howToFix: Calculate the missed years of Zakat according to one's best estimation and pay it immediately to valid recipients. Keep proper accounting. Seek forgiveness for the delay.
evidenceQuran: And those who hoard gold and silver and spend it not in the way of Allah - give them tidings of a painful punishment. The Day when it will be heated in the fire of Hell...
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever is made wealthy by Allah and does not pay the Zakat of his wealth, then on the Day of Resurrection his wealth will be made like a bald-headed poisonous male snake..."

title: Sariqah (Theft & Robbery)
consequences: Loss of faith during the act. Deprivation of Barakah. Serious public punishment in an Islamic state, and severe accounting in the Hereafter if the rights are not restored to the victim.
reasonsToAvoid: It violently breaches the safety of society and destroys mutual trust. It shows an extreme lack of contentment with what Allah has provisioned (Rizq).
howToFix: Tawbah is not accepted for theft until the stolen property is returned to its rightful owner. If the owner cannot be found, the equivalent amount must be given in charity on their behalf.
evidenceQuran: [As for] the thief, the male and the female, amputate their hands in recompense for what they committed as a deterrent [punishment] from Allah.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Allah curses the thief who steals an egg, for which his hand is cut off, and who steals a rope, for which his hand is cut off."

title: Al-Kadhib (Lying)
consequences: Lying leads to wickedness, and wickedness leads to the Hellfire. A habitual liar will have their face torn from the corners of their mouth to the back of their head repeatedly in the Barzakh (grave).
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys personal honor, societal trust, and is cowardly. It contradicts the core Islamic value of Truth (Al-Haqq).
howToFix: Constantly speak the truth even against oneself. Make Tawbah for past lies, and if a lie caused direct harm or false testimony (slander), one must return to the people and clarify the truth to clear the victim's name.
evidenceQuran: O you who have believed, fear Allah and be with those who are true.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise... And falsehood leads to Al-Fajur (wickedness), and Al-Fajur leads to the Hellfire."

title: Hasad (Envy & Jealousy)
consequences: Complete loss of inner peace, physical sickness, and depression in this world. The forfeiture of mountains of good deeds in the Hereafter.
reasonsToAvoid: It poisons relationships and blinds a person to the blessings Allah has given them. It transforms the heart into a resentful, dark vessel.
howToFix: Combat Hasad by doing the exact opposite: make sincere Dua for the person you envy that Allah increases their blessing. Give them gifts. Praise them when your ego wants to criticize them. Recognize that Allah's bounty is infinite.
evidenceQuran: Or do they envy people for what Allah has given them of His bounty?
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Beware of envy, for envy consumes good deeds just as fire consumes wood."

title: Rishwah (Bribery)
consequences: Invites the curse of Allah in this world, rendering prayers unanswered due to Haram consumption. Destroys justice, oppresses the poor, and places incompetent people in power, leading to societal collapse.
reasonsToAvoid: It fundamentally violates the principle of Justice (Adl). It allows the wealthy to bypass the law and steal the rights of the weak.
howToFix: Cease paying bribes. Return bribery money taken if you are an official. Build systems of transparency. Depend on Allah (Tawakkul) to grant your needs through Halal channels rather than taking the corrupt shortcut.
evidenceQuran: And do not consume one another's wealth unjustly or send it [in bribery] to the rulers in order that [they might aid] you [to] consume a portion of the wealth of the people in sin, while you know [it is unlawful].
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The curse of Allah is upon the one who offers a bribe and the one who takes it."

title: Qati'at al-Rahm (Severing Ties of Kinship)
description: Cutting What Allah Ordered to be Joined
consequences: The person who severs kinship will NOT enter Paradise. Their lifespan and wealth will lose Barakah, and their deeds are rejected every Thursday night.
reasonsToAvoid: Allah explicitly cursed those who spread corruption on earth and sever their ties of kinship. It breaks the foundational unit of society.
howToFix: Initiate the greeting (Salam), even if they reject you. Forgive past grudges for the sake of Allah. Visit them, give them gifts, and make Dua for them. If they are abusive, maintain a safe, distant, but polite connection without severing entirely.
evidenceQuran: Would you then, if you were given the authority, do mischief in the land, and sever your ties of kinship? Such are they whom Allah has cursed, so that He has made them deaf and blinded their sight.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The severer of the tie of kinship will not enter Paradise."

title: Suicide (Qatl al-Nafs)
description: Despair in the Mercy of Allah
consequences: The Prophet (SAW) warned that whoever kills himself with something in this world will be severely punished with that exact same thing repeatedly in the fire of Hell.
reasonsToAvoid: Worldly pain is temporary and serves to expiate sins. Ending one's life rejects the divine decree, hurts the family immensely, and trades temporary worldly pain for severe punishment in the Hereafter.
howToFix: Seek immediate clinical and psychiatric help. Build Tawakkul (trust) that Allah does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear. Hold firmly to the rope of prayer and know that with hardship comes ease.
evidenceQuran: And do not kill yourselves [or one another]. Indeed, Allah is to you ever Merciful.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever throws himself down from a mountain and kills himself will be throwing himself down in the Fire... forever."

title: Al-Ma'azif (Musical Instruments)
consequences: Music acts as a direct competitor to the Quran in the heart; they cannot coexist. It breeds hypocrisy, incites illicit desires (Zina), and wastes the precious time Allah gave for worship.
reasonsToAvoid: The modern music industry normalizes drugs, violence, illicit sex, and Kufr. Listening to it severely weakens one's concentration (Khushu) in Salah.
howToFix: Replace music with the recitation of the Quran, beneficial podcasts, or purely vocal Nasheeds. Delete playlists of Haraam music. The urge to listen to it will fade as the heart fills with the tranquility of the Quran.
evidenceQuran: And of the people is he who buys the amusement of speech to mislead [others] from the way of Allah without knowledge...
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "There will be among my Ummah people who will regard as permissible illegal sexual intercourse, the wearing of silk (for men), the drinking of alcoholic drinks, and the use of musical instruments."

title: Tabarruj (Displaying Beauty)
consequences: It brings the curse of Allah and His angels. Women who display their charms to strangers and "are clothed yet naked" will not smell the fragrance of Paradise, which can be smelled from vast distances.
reasonsToAvoid: It degrades women into objects of desire. It sparks societal sedition (Fitnah), destroys marriages, and disobeys the direct command of the Creator, who defined what true dignity is.
howToFix: Adhere to the proper Islamic dress code (Hijab: loose, opaque, covering everything except the face and hands). Seek validation from Allah rather than the fleeting attention of the society.
evidenceQuran: And abide in your houses and do not display yourselves as [was] the display of the former times of ignorance.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) mentioned two types of the people of Hellfire... "and women who are clothed yet naked, walking with an enticing gait... they will not enter Paradise, nor smell its fragrance."

title: Murder (Qatl)
consequences: Whoever intentionally kills a believer, their recompense is Hell to abide therein forever. The wrath and curse of Allah are upon them. On the Day of Judgment, the victim will drag the murderer by the neck to Allah's throne demanding justice.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys the ultimate foundational right given by Allah: the right to life. It usurps Allah's exclusive authority over life and death and shatters families and societies.
howToFix: If it was intentional, the murderer must turn themselves into the Islamic authorities to accept Qisas (execution) or pay blood money if the family forgives. Sincere Tawbah is required. If undisclosed, one must repent and do massive amounts of good deeds to weigh against the victim's claim on Judgment Day.
evidenceQuran: Because of that, We decreed upon the Children of Israel that whoever kills a soul unless for a soul or for corruption [done] in the land - it is as if he had slain mankind entirely.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The first cases to be decided among the people on the Day of Resurrection will be those of blood-shed."

title: Fraud in Business (Tatfeef)
consequences: Woe and destruction to the defrauders. The wealth obtained through fraud will become a source of torment in this life and the fire of Hell in the next.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys the economic trust of a society. A society where no one trusts the merchants becomes a society of paranoia, stagnation, and mutual exploitation.
howToFix: Return the defrauded amounts to the customers immediately. If they cannot be found, give the equivalent in charity without expecting a reward. Be completely transparent about defects when selling in the future.
evidenceQuran: Woe to those who give less [than due], Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full. But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) passed by a pile of food in the market. He put his hand inside it and felt dampness. He said: "Whoever deceives us is not one of us."

title: Swearing by Other Than Allah
consequences: Minor Shirk is worse than all other major sins (like drinking or stealing) combined, because it infringes upon the rights of Tawhid (Monotheism).
reasonsToAvoid: Swearing by an entity elevates that entity to a level of ultimate reverence that is reserved solely for the Creator of the Heavens and Earth.
howToFix: Stop doing it immediately. If it slips off the tongue out of habit, immediately follow it with "La ilaha illallah" (There is no god but Allah) to correct the creedal slip.
evidenceQuran: And do not make [your oath by] Allah an excuse against being righteous and fearing Allah and making peace among people.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever swears by other than Allah has committed an act of disbelief or polytheism."

title: Imitating the Opposite Gender
consequences: Being cursed by the Prophet means being distanced entirely from the Mercy of Allah in both worlds. It leads to psychological fracturing and societal collapse of family roles.
reasonsToAvoid: It is a direct objection to the infinite wisdom of Allah, who chose to create the individual as male or female for specific spiritual and societal purposes.
howToFix: Embrace the gender Allah created you upon. Men should adopt the clothing, responsibilities, and protective nature of men. Women should adopt the clothing, dignity, and nurturing nature of women. Make Tawbah for past imitation.
evidenceQuran: And do not wish for that by which Allah has made some of you exceed others. For men is a share of what they have earned, and for women is a share of what they have earned.
evidenceHadith: The Messenger of Allah (SAW) cursed the men who imitate women and the women who imitate men.

title: Tattoos & Body Modification
consequences: The person who performs the modification and the person who asks for it are both cursed by Allah. Their prayers may be affected if the tattoo prevents water from reaching the skin during Wudu (though most modern tattoos are sub-dermal).
reasonsToAvoid: It stems from dissatisfaction with the perfect design of the Creator. Satan explicitly swore to Allah that he would command humans to alter Allah's creation.
howToFix: If a person gets a tattoo before accepting Islam, or due to ignorance, they are forgiven via their repentance. If it can be removed easily (like via laser), it should be. If removal causes severe harm or burning, they should leave it, seek forgiveness, and their prayer is perfectly valid.
evidenceQuran: [Satan said] "And I will mislead them, and I will arouse in them [sinful] desires, and I will command them so they will slit the ears of cattle, and I will command them so they will change the creation of Allah."
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) cursed the women who practice tattooing and those who seek to be tattooed, the women who remove hair from their faces, and those who create spaces between their teeth for beauty, altering the creation of Allah.

title: Watching Pornography
consequences: It destroys Khushu (concentration) in prayer. It removes the light (Noor) from a person's face. It causes erectile dysfunction, marital collapse, and severe depression due to dopamine receptor burnout.
reasonsToAvoid: It trains the brain to view other human beings merely as sexual objects rather than souls. It creates unrealistic expectations that destroy the beauty and intimacy of a real, halal Islamic marriage.
howToFix: Install strict blockers on all devices. Never be alone with a device late at night. Fast to lower desires. Make sincere Tawbah. If addicted, seek clinical Muslim therapy focused on addiction recovery.
evidenceQuran: Tell the believing men to reduce [some] of their vision and guard their private parts. That is purer for them. Indeed, Allah is Acquainted with what they do.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The adultery of the eyes is looking (at forbidden things), the adultery of the ears is listening, the adultery of the tongue is speaking..."

title: Masturbation (Istimna')
consequences: Spiritual weakness, loss of confidence, and intense feelings of guilt and distance from Allah. It often requires Ghusl (ritual bath), leading people to miss their prayers out of laziness or shame to perform Ghusl frequently.
reasonsToAvoid: It breaks the boundary Allah set for sexual fulfillment. It feeds the ego and isolates the individual, whereas Islam seeks to channel that natural energy into building a family through marriage.
howToFix: The prophetic cure is marriage. If marriage is not financially or logistically possible, the Prophet commanded fasting, which lowers the libido. Avoid isolation, lower the gaze, and engage in intense physical exercise.
evidenceQuran: And they who guard their private parts - Except from their wives... But whoever seeks beyond that, then those are the transgressors.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "O young men, whoever among you can afford it, let him get married... and whoever cannot afford it should fast, for it will be a shield for him."

title: Free Mixing (Ikhtilat)
consequences: It is the primary gateway to Zina. The gradual erosion of shyness (Haya) leads first to emotional attachment, then to physical sins. It destroys marriages through emotional affairs.
reasonsToAvoid: Islam is proactive, not just reactive. It blocks the avenues that lead to sin. By preventing continuous casual mixing, the sanctity of families is preserved and desires are protected from constant triggering.
howToFix: Keep interactions with the opposite gender strictly professional and brief. Do not isolate yourself with a non-Mahram. In workplaces, maintain a respectful distance and lower the gaze.
evidenceQuran: And when you ask [his wives] for something, ask them from behind a partition. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "No man is alone with a (non-Mahram) woman but the Shaytaan is the third one present."

title: Namimah (Tale-bearing)
consequences: The tale-bearer will suffer torment in the grave. The Prophet stated explicitly that the one who spreads malice and gossip to cause rifts will NOT enter Paradise.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys families, splits communities, and acts as the ultimate weapon of Satan to cause hatred between believers.
howToFix: If someone brings you malicious gossip about what someone else said, immediately reject it, advise the tale-bearer to fear Allah, and do not pass it on. Make Tawbah if you have spread tales and try to reconcile the people you divided.
evidenceQuran: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer, [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The tale-bearer (Namim) shall not enter Paradise."

title: Su' al-Zann (Evil Suspicion)
consequences: It drives a person to spy on others, backbite them, and isolate themselves. The Prophet called unfounded suspicion the "worst of false tales."
reasonsToAvoid: It replaces clear evidence with Satanic whispers. It breeds a toxic, paranoid community where trust is impossible.
howToFix: Assume the absolute best of a Muslim's actions. If you see them do something questionable, interpret it in the best possible light. Guard your heart against the whispers of Shaytaan.
evidenceQuran: O you who have believed, avoid much [negative] assumption. Indeed, some assumption is sin. And do not spy or backbite each other.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales; and do not look for the others' faults and do not spy..."

title: Consuming Orphan's Wealth
consequences: The Quran states explicitly that those who consume the wealth of orphans unjustly are merely swallowing pure fire into their bellies, and they will burn in a blazing Fire.
reasonsToAvoid: The orphan is uniquely vulnerable. Oppressing the most defenseless member of society brings the immediate and severe wrath of the Most Merciful.
howToFix: Immediately return the wealth to the orphan. Ask for their forgiveness once they are of age. Make intense repentance to Allah for betraying the trust of the vulnerable.
evidenceQuran: Indeed, those who devour the property of orphans unjustly are only consuming into their bellies fire. And they will be burned in a Blaze.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Avoid the seven great destructive sins... (including) devouring the property of an orphan."

title: Fleeing the Battlefield
consequences: The one who flees draws upon himself the extreme wrath of Allah, and their destination is Hellfire—an evil destination. It is considered an internal betrayal of the highest order in Islam.
reasonsToAvoid: The physical defense of the Ummah relies on the steadfastness of the defenders. Fleeing out of cowardice throws the lives of the remaining brothers, as well as the women and children behind the lines, into total jeopardy.
howToFix: If one fled in the past, they must make sincere, weeping repentance to Allah. True courage comes from knowing that the time of death is already written and cannot be delayed or advanced by running.
evidenceQuran: O you who have believed, when you meet those who disbelieve advancing [for battle], do not turn to them your backs [in flight].
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Avoid the seven great destructive sins... (including) turning one's back to the enemy and fleeing from the battlefield at the time of fighting."

title: Slandering Chaste Women (Qazf)
consequences: It is one of the seven destructive sins. The spreader of such rumors is cursed in this world and the Hereafter, and faces a "great punishment" on the day when their tongues, hands, and feet will testify against them.
reasonsToAvoid: It destroys lives, causes divorces, sparks blood feuds, and ruins the psychological well-being of innocent believers. It turns a society into a factory of vile rumors.
howToFix: Stop the rumor immediately. Go to the people you told, confess you were lying or had no proof, and clear the victim's name entirely. Seek forgiveness from the person you slandered and from Allah.
evidenceQuran: Indeed, those who [falsely] accuse chaste, unaware and believing women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter; and they will have a great punishment.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Avoid the seven great destructive sins... (including) slandering chaste, innocent, believing women."

title: False Testimony (Shahadat al-Zoor)
consequences: Severe punishment in Hell. The feet of a false witness will not move from their place on the Day of Judgment until Hellfire is made mandatory for them.
reasonsToAvoid: It is the complete subversion of Adl (Justice). An Islamic society is built entirely on truth. False testimony weaponizes the court system, turning a place of safety into an instrument of tyranny.
howToFix: Recant the false testimony immediately, even if it results in personal consequence. Restore whatever rights or wealth were lost due to the lie. Ask for Allah's forgiveness sincerely.
evidenceQuran: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for Allah, even if it be against yourselves or parents and relatives.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) sat up from reclining and said: "Shall I not tell you of the biggest of the great sins? They are: To join others in worship with Allah, to be undutiful to one's parents, and I warn you against giving a false statement and a false witness." He kept on repeating that warning till we wished he would stop.

title: Abandoning Hajj When Able
consequences: Umar ibn al-Khattab considered imposing the Jizya (tax on non-Muslims) on those who had the means to perform Hajj but did not, implying they lacked complete faith. Delaying it risks dying before fulfilling a core pillar.
reasonsToAvoid: Hajj is the ultimate physical and financial sacrifice for Allah. Refusing it shows a deep attachment to worldly wealth and comfort over obedience to the Creator's direct invitation to His House.
howToFix: Make the intention and begin preparations mathematically and logistically today. If one delayed it and grew too sick or old, they must pay for someone else (who has already done their own Hajj) to perform it on their behalf.
evidenceQuran: And [due] to Allah from the people is a pilgrimage to the House - for whoever is able to find thereto a way. But whoever disbelieves - then indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Hasten to perform the Hajj (the obligatory one), for none of you knows what may happen to him."

title: Not Fasting in Ramadan
consequences: The Prophet (SAW) was shown a vision in Hell where people were hung by their hamstrings, their mouths torn open and bleeding profusely. He was told: "These are the people who broke their fasts before the time."
reasonsToAvoid: Ramadan is the month of mercy and the Quran. Willfully eating in it shows total disregard for the sacredness of Allah's chosen month and destroys the Taqwa (consciousness of Allah) that fasting is meant to build.
howToFix: If missed intentionally, one must sincerely repent out of fear of Allah. They must make up (Qada) the specific days missed. They should heavily engage in voluntary fasting (like Mondays and Thursdays) to make up for the deficiency.
evidenceQuran: The month of Ramadhan [is that] in which was revealed the Qur'an... So whoever sights [the new moon of] the month, let him fast it.
evidenceHadith: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Islam is built upon five pillars... and fasting Ramadan."
